extraction.one_line_summary: JAKOBSEN Fabio won the sprint after ALPECIN-FENIX went extremely early trying to force rivals to commit, DEMARE took the bait but JAKOBSEN sat longer.
tactical_patterns: ALPECIN-FENIX deliberately sprinted early from distance to force premature responses, tactical gambit failed against disciplined JAKOBSEN wheel.
DEMARE Arnold required perfect leadout on wide boulevards without technical corners to compete with pure power sprinters.
PARAME Rein crashed post-3km barrier after JUMBO rider crossed wheels, revealing peloton positioning vulnerability despite time neutralization rule.

rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: positive: First GT stage post-Poland crash shows elite sprint power still intact despite psychological recovery concerns
rider_signals.DEMARE Arnold: positive: Strong second suggests resurgence on technical-free finishes where positioning chaos doesn't nullify his leadout advantage
rider_signals.CORT Magnus: neutral: Third place indicates sprinting capability but lacks acceleration to challenge top-tier finishers
rider_signals.PARAME Rein: negative: Crash exposed poor peloton positioning awareness in final 3km despite holding race lead
